name: every rule emitted by standards-refresh.sh aligns with an enforcement surface as a third-party tool a semantic native fallback or an explicit operator sign-off flag
command:
OUT=/tmp/cl564-r47-$$; mkdir -p $OUT/out && printf '

Encrypt S3 buckets at rest in Terraform

Every aws_s3_bucket terraform resource MUST configure server_side_encryption_configuration with AES256 or KMS.

Retain operational records

Records must be retained for the mandated period.

Module style preference

You should prefer readable style in modules.

' > $OUT/s.html && cat > $OUT/reg.yaml <e.err; T=$OUT/out/us-government/r47-src.yaml; python3 -c " import re, sys t = open('$T').read() blocks = re.findall(r'^- id:.*?(?=^- id:|^recommended_set:)', t, re.M | re.S) assert len(blocks) == 3, 'expected 3 rules' for b in blocks: tooled = re.search(r'^ - tool: (?!prose-judge)', b, re.M) native = '- tool: prose-judge.sh' in b or '- bundle: architectural-content' in b signoff = 'needs_operator_signoff: true' in b assert tooled or native or signoff, 'unaligned rule: ' + b[:60] s3 = [b for b in blocks if 'encrypt-s3-buckets' in b][0] assert re.search(r'^ - tool: (checkov|trivy)', s3, re.M), 's3 rule must route to a third-party tool' advisory = [b for b in blocks if 'module-style-preference' in b][0] assert 'needs_operator_signoff: true' in advisory, 'advisory rule must be flagged for sign-off' retain = [b for b in blocks if 'retain-operational-records' in b][0] assert '- tool: prose-judge.sh' in retain, 'untooled rule must bind the semantic native fallback' print('ok') " && echo every-rule-enforcement-aligned 1>&2; rm -rf $OUT
